programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness cook their main courses, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Phil with roast loin of lamb with pie and mash, carrots, nettles and mint; Graham with slow cooked marsh lamb breast, rasher and spiced rissole; or Marcus with blade of beef cooked in hay, tendons and beetroot?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their dessert dishes here. Will it be Phil with rhubarb and custard souffle, Marcus with asparagus, goats curd and black olive, or Graham with lemon meringue? Veteran Jason Atherton decides who goes through to the judges and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from the London and South East region. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. The chefs task is to create a menu which captures the Olympic spirit.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle of the Wales chefs: returning contenders Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry. All are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. Stephen Terry cooks `The Opening Ceremony, Richard Davies makes ravioli of quail, parfait of foie gras, roasted salsify and Madeira jelly, and James Sommerin cooks sage cream, onion, chicken and Welsh `Brie truffle toast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry are all determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. They cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Fish and Shellfish Medley, Richard Davies with compression of vine tomato, poached lobster and hand dived scallop, or James Sommerin with lobster and Iberico ham, spiced butter and broccoli?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their main course, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Bunny Pentathlon, Richard Davies with slow cooked rib of beef, beetroot ketchup, bone marrow and braised shallot, or James Sommerin with mangalitsa pork, carrot, liquorice and leek?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their dessert, but who will impress? Stephen Terry with `Bronze, Silver or Gold?, Richard Davies with passion fruit cream and gel with bitter chocolate sorbet or James Sommerin with raspberry and lemon cheesecake? `Great British Menu veteran Angela Hartnett decides who goes through to the judges on Friday and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from Wales. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West contenders Paul Ainsworth and Nathan Outlaw, plus newcomer Simon Hulstone, kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Paul with his `Breakfast of Champions; Nathan with hogs pudding with seaweed, potato terrine and mushroom ketchup; or Simon Hulstone with track and field - beetroot and goats cheese?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish course, but which one but which one will prevail? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with monkfish `Two Showings; Nathan Outlaw with mackerel, horseradish and oyster sauce; or Simon Hulstone with `The Swimming Pool?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course, but which chef will take gold? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Wishful Chicken; Nathan Outlaw with duck, barbecue monkfish, rosemary, samphire and asparagus; or Simon Hulstone with lamb with salted toffee pea mousse, shallot puree and asparagus?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs final chance to impress the veteran with their dessert dishes. Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Then and Now; Nathan Outlaw with elderflower and lemon tart, with strawberry sorbet and meringues; or Simon Hulstone with `Closing Ceremony?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the South West region. One chef left the competition on the previous day and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The 2012 competition enters its final week. The winners of the regional heats compete for a chance for their dish to be on the final banquet menu at a prestigious event hosted by Sir Steve Redgrave with a glittering guest list of British Olympians. Here, the finalists cook their starters, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Richard Corrigan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight winners of the regional heats prepare to cook their fish dishes, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Marcus Wareing. Before they get into the kitchen, they must first learn whether they will all be cooking.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: On day three, the chosen finalists cook their main courses, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Tom Kerridge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its desserts day in the finals, and the last chance for the competing chefs to impress the judges and veteran Angela Hartnett. After a hard-fought week, the end of this programme will see the judges finally reveal the winning four dishes which will make up this years `Great British Menu.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This final programme follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the Olympic banquet at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, as they make the painstaking preparations for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook. The four winning chefs prepare an outstanding four-course menu for a truly Olympian guest list.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline ODonnell and Neil Rankin go head to head to represent Scotland. Today they must impress the mystery veteran judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs, double Michelin starred Stevie McLaughlin, experienced Jacqueline ODonnell and newcomer Neil Rankin fight it out for the fish course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course for Scottish chefs Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline ODonnell and Neil Rankin. With the scores close, the pressure is on for all three and theyre each hoping to impress with unique and individual dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the Scottish chefs fates hang in the balance as at the end of today, one of them must be sent home. For Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline ODonnell and Neil Rankin theres no room for error.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs fight it out for just one place in the finals, cooking all four of their dishes again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Also scoring the menus is guest judge and guest of honour Jim Radford.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the South Wests finest chefs, returning contender Emily Watkins, experienced Dominic Chapman and newcomer Josh Eggleton fight it out. Today they must cook their starter courses to be judged by the surprise veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs, returner Emily Watkins and Michelin starred Dominic Chapman and Josh Eggleton do battle for the fish course. Dom has been inspired by his research trip to the scene of the D Day landings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs Emily Watkins, Dominic Chapman and Josh Eggleton cook their main courses for the veteran judge. With the competition so tight can one of them pull out in front before the deciding dessert course?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the South West chefs last chance to secure a place in the regional final. Two will make it through to cook their menus for the judges but either Emily Watkins, Dominic Chapman or Josh Eggleton must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two South West chefs left standing cook their four courses again for the judges. Theyre joined by special guest judge and hero of the D-Day landings, 93-year-old war veteran Ken Sturdy, a Royal Navy signalman in World War II.

programme.desc: Three of Wales top chefs, returning contender Mary Ann Gilchrist, former military man David Kelman and fellow newcomer Andy Beaumont compete for the last remaining place in the finals.

programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the Welsh chefs last chance to impress. After David Kelmans main course result, Mary Ann Gilchrist and Andy Beaumont must do everything they can not to crack under the pressure.

programme.desc: The story of a couple who believe their baby is the target of an evil spirit; a woman claims she is attacked by dark entities in her home; paranormal investigators tell a man that a ghost wants to kill him.

programme.title: Scariest Night of My Life

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: Rob and Monique speculate about the ghost of the black-robed judge that haunted their first house.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters

programme.title: Quest for the Lost Tribes
programme.desc: Filmmaker Simcha Jacobovic searches the globe for answers and investigates the lost tribes of Israel. From 12 mentioned in the Bible only Judah and Benjamin remain after they survived the Assyrian purge of nearly three millennia ago.

programme.title: The Story of Egyptology
programme.desc: Dr. Chris Naunton delves into the rediscovery of Ancient Egypt, tracing its discovery from early travelers.

programme.title: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: A kingdom of vast statues, towering obelisks and temples was the legacy left by the greatest Egyptian Emperor. Cutting-edge graphics recreate Ramses golden empire.

programme.title: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: Bringing ancient cities and monuments, lost to history, back to life. In this episode - an exploration of the network of secret tunnels that kept the British government safe and operational during WWII. Just how far did this underground citadel go?

programme.title: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: Reconstructing the Jerusalem of 20 centuries ago.

programme.title: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: The fictitious vampire Dracula was based on a real Romanian prince, famous for both his cruelty and courage. The astounding empire built by the fearsome warlord is recreated.

programme.title: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: The US had to beat the Nazis in the race to create an atomic bomb. They built many secret nuclear facilities. Here, the controversial sites are recreated.

programme.title: 1805: The Battle of Austerlitz
programme.desc: Recounting an important day in December of 1805, which became one of the most decisive battles and the greatest victory of the Napoleonic era.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: Hitlers Secret Weapons Manager
programme.desc: Did the man behind Hitlers secret weapons programme survive the war? Was SS General Hans Kammler covertly brought into the USA to safeguard his knowledge? New evidence on a vexing enigma.

programme.title: Secrets Of Hitlers Island Fortress
programme.desc: Guernsey, the only British isle occupied by Nazi Germany during WWII, reveals unique wartime history.

programme.title: Hitlers Jurassic Monsters
programme.desc: Dramatic reconstructions show how Goerings ethnic cleansing went hand in hand with his attempts to actually recreate extinct Germanic beasts like the Aurochs.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: What persuaded one-time "Man of the Year" Pierre Laval to send Jews to the death camps?

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Uncovers the motives behind the people and organisations who made the choice to aid the Nazi war effort.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Viktor Arajs formed a Latvian commando unit that hunted Jews.

programme.title: The Mistake that Killed Hitler
programme.desc: In April 1945, as Stalins Red Army approach from the east and the western Allied forces quickly approach through France and Belgium, Adolf Hitler awaits his fate in his bunker, reflecting on the mistakes that lost him the war.

programme.title: Great American Generals - Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, Bradley
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage provides insight into what made Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, and Bradley heroes of American history.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives
programme.desc: The Nazi regime lasted from 1933 to 1945 and was one of the most horrific periods of history. However, Adolf Hitler was not working alone. He had a circle of some of the most barbaric men alongside him, who helped make the atrocities possible.

programme.title: The Fall of France
programme.desc: Explores the stage of World War Two where the Germans defeated France in a blistering invasion.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: Hitlers Secret Weapons Manager
programme.desc: Did the man behind Hitlers secret weapons programme survive the war? Was SS General Hans Kammler covertly brought into the USA to safeguard his knowledge? New evidence on a vexing enigma.

programme.title: Hitlers Secret Weapons Manager
programme.desc: Did the man behind Hitlers secret weapons programme survive the war? Was SS General Hans Kammler covertly brought into the USA to safeguard his knowledge? New evidence on a vexing enigma.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: After hearing about his great-grandfather who served in the Merchant Navy, Ross Kemp is inspired to explore the wreck of a WWII cargo ship and try to recover its military supplies.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Inspired by the story of his two cousins that died at sea in WWII, Ross Kemp explores captured German scout warship, the F2 and brings up treasure that could link back to his relatives.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The Kaiser Sunken Fleet: Ross Kemp continues his adventure in Scapa Flow, plunging down on the wrecks of a German warship and an experimental submarine aircraft called the M2. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Situated high on a crag overlooking the river Forth, Stirling Castle has guarded the gateway to the Scottish Highlands for centuries. The residence of kings, armies and felons, its battle-scarred walls have witnessed savagery and chivalry, intrigue, alchemy, adultery and murder. Location filming, stunning aerial shots and reconstructions bring to to life its turbulent history.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Elizabethan Plymouth, exploring the story of Englands `Golden Age, from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Georgian Edinburghs finest architecture, the origins of `Auld Reekie, the roots of modern Scottishness, and some dark imperial controversies.

programme.title: Truthseekers
programme.desc: This ornate, gold-plated chest housed the 10 commandments given to Moses by God and is said to have been built some 3,000 years ago.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Using homemade instruments to carve a hole in the roof after climbing prison pipes, eight inmates escaped from a New Mexico detention centre in 2008.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Stone Age Engineering and Battle of Bullecourt
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2018s best archaeological finds from the east of the UK: a monument as old as Stonehenge, a dig at the site of Britains first tank battle, and some disturbing Roman burials.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts follows the excavation of Iron Age Britains most spectacular grave. A team of archaeologists in East Yorkshire have uncovered the remains of only the third upright chariot burial ever found in Britain.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Situated high on a crag overlooking the river Forth, Stirling Castle has guarded the gateway to the Scottish Highlands for centuries. programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her hometown of Bristol, from architectural gems to the worlds first chocolate factory, backstreet gin and the horror of the slave trade.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on his most curious walk yet, across the ancient and turbulent kingdom of Northumbria. In the seventh century, at the height of the Dark Ages, the rule of Northumbria stretched from Edinburgh to York.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the wonderful seascapes of the North Norfolk coast and through one of the least developed spots in the country.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Focusing on Roman Britannia, where finds include the thickening mystery of 97 baby skeletons found by the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon that turns history on its head, and a Roman cult figure buried for 1700 years beneath a fort.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. She looks for signs of the earliest Viking settlers in the Outer Hebrides, and in Orkney - where Viking dominance outlasted anywhere else in Britain - she visits the excavation of a Viking chiefs citadel and finds evidence of their way-of-life.

programme.title: Truthseekers
programme.desc: Explore the huge Nazca lines that were made over 2,000 years ago and are still a mystery to researchers today.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Mafia and Britain
programme.desc: Ross Kemp investigates and exposes Britains surprising and deep-rooted links with the mafia, dating as far back as the 1920s.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: After scaling walls and eluding officers to escape from three separate high security prisons in the 50s, Alfie Hinds earned the nickname Houdini.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Using homemade instruments to carve a hole in the roof after climbing prison pipes, eight inmates escaped from a New Mexico detention centre in 2008.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson is out to discover why Pembrokeshire feels so English. There are castles everywhere and it boasts one of the countrys finest cathedrals, sure signs that the Normans stamped their mark all over this little slice of Wales.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the British Channel Islands, which were occupied by Nazi Germany for five years between 1940 and 1945.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chiropodist, two part-time gardeners and a woman who works on projects in between the school run are todays investors. Theyve bought property in Bullbridge in Derbyshire, Newcastle under Lyme in Staffordshire and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts follows the progress of a couple developing a chalet bungalow in Drakelowe in Staffordshire for their family, while Lucy Alexander talks to a young entrepreneur with grand plans for a three-bed semi in Croydon in London. Dion Dublin looks round a piece of land which runs alongside a tourist steam train track in Coltishall in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts rolls back the clock nearly 12 years to catch up on an ambitious development of a former church in Preston on the Hill in Cheshire, while Lucy Alexander looks round two very different terraced houses in Stoney Stratford, near Milton Keynes, and Streatham in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s terrace in Croydon, a semi in Wolstanton destined to be a family home and the only house on a street in Moorside in Oldham are all under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion visit the properties and meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 20-year-old first-time developer, a banker turned developer and a builder who wants to create a new home for his soon-to-be-wife all buy properties at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion are in Chesterton, Hornchurch and Sudbrook to hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down listed village house in Farningham in Kent is given the wow factor while houses in Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire are also given great makeovers. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and catch up on their progress after the transformations.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent. Meanwhile, Martin meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A commercial and residential building in Beeston in Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich in London and a flat in Godalming in Surrey are all given high-end makeovers. Will the developers, who all bought at auction, make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertforshire, a flat in Croydon and a workshop and land in Heanor in Derbyshire are sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the owners and hear their plans to make money from their bricks and mortar.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mum and dad buy a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire in the hope of developing a pair of houses for their children, a couple who buy a top-floor flat in Motherwell in Scotland want to make a profit on the rent, and two young brothers buy a run-down house in South Normanton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Langton Green in Kent is given a high-end refurbishment and extension, a father-and-son team cleverly refigure the existing space in a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and two lifelong friends have high hopes for a piece of land they buy behind a house they already own in Northwood in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Leamington Spa in Warwickshire, a terraced house in Londons Plumstead and a workshop on the Isle of Sheppey in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin hear about the plans for the three properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on ambitious plans to redevelop the site of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, while Dion Dublin talks to a first-time developer planning to extend up and out at a semi in Bloxwich in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Sutton in London, a studio apartment in Chatham in Kent and a terraced house in Middlesbrough are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Biggin Hill with huge potential, a centuries-old cottage in a beautiful part of Devon and an end terrace crying out for an upstairs bathroom are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell joins the team, and looks at an old mews house in an historic part of Litchfield in Staffordshire, while Martin Roberts assesses the potential of a bungalow in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks at a large terraced house in Dover in Kent with bags of potential. Martin Roberts is in Camborne in Cornwall viewing a house with lots of issues. Dion Dublin meets a couple who bought a flat in Woodford Green in Essex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in Gillingham in Kent meeting a couple embarking on their first renovation development, Martin Roberts talks to a man with ambitious plans for a house in Londons Chingford, and Dion Dublin looks round a semi in Wolverhampton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell hears development plans for a two-bedroom flat in Godalming in Surrey, Martin Roberts looks at a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford in Shropshire, and Dion Dublin sizes up the work needing to be done at a terraced house in Smethwick in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with great sea views in Margate in Kent, a bungalow in Whaley Thorns in Nottinghamshire and an end terrace in Longbridge in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A listed building in Brentwood in Essex dating back to the 16th-century is sold under the hammer, along with a flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

Face is shot during the struggle and requires urgent medical treatment, but when he and Murdock learn that the hitmen plan to kill the Attorney General, they face a battle to warn Hannibal and BA in time.

programme.title: The Invaders
programme.desc: David Vincent learns that the aliens have been using radio-implants to `program human beings.

programme.title: The 7 Adventures of Sinbad
programme.desc: Persian prince Sinbad is charged with completing seven seemingly-impossible tasks in order to save humanity from an imminent catastrophe.

programme.title: Legend Lowdown

programme.title: The Buccaneer

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: Islanders are besieged by mutants that live on human bone.

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.sub-title: Let That Be Your Last Battlefield

programme.title: The Invaders
programme.desc: An architect is convinced there are aliens in the world.

programme.title: T.J. Hooker

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex ventures out on a camping trip but is kidnapped by a pair of brothers, triggering a rescue effort from Walker and Trivette.

programme.title: Maximum Risk
programme.desc: High-octane action thriller about a retired French soldiers search for his twin brothers killers. Unaware that he even had a twin until he found him dead on the streets of his home town, the policeman vows not only to avenge the murder, but also to uncover the truth about his own past. The trail soon leads him to a Russian syndicate based in New York.

programme.title: Nowhere to Run
programme.desc: An escaped convict strikes up a relationship with a widow and her two children who are being harassed by unscrupulous land developers headed by the evil Franklin Hale. Having virtually become a father to the children, the fugitive is determined to help the family see off Hale and his henchmen for good.

programme.title: Best of the Best
programme.desc: Martial arts drama in which a misfit team of American kick-boxers must undergo rigorous training for an all-important contest against a ruthless Korean team. Along the way, the hardened fighters learn to lay their personal ghosts to rest.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first to host is Mrs Tee of Gorse Meadow, a four star BandB in the New Forest. Her eccentric personality charms the guests on arrival but not everyones room is up to scratch. John and Neils bathroom window needs fixing and Alistair and Emma Mundell find they are sharing their room with ladybirds, cobwebs and dust.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien revamp and repurpose hidden treasures. Former agony aunt Janets Suffolk shed is crammed with goodies, including furniture and a vintage tram bench.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Andi and Neil dream of retiring to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Can Laura Hamilton find their perfect place close to the coastal town of Mazarron, near Murcia, for £145,000?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The weeks competition is in and around Belfast, where first to host is chatty PR manager Claire. She aims to impress with a menu made up of her favourite restaurant dishes, but never having plucked a pheasant before she has a challenge on her hands.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second day in and around Belfast, its the turn of carpenter and country boy Neil to do his best to land this weeks £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Third host from Belfast is 57-year-old window cleaning business owner and keen golfer Paul. Debonair Paul has cut a dash already this week with his sense of style and now hes hoping to impress with his fine food.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hosting in and around Belfast is livewire of the group, 28-year-old DJ and events organiser Ryan. With the bar set high in the competition, the pressure is on for the man who has hosted events for thousands.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night of competition in Belfast and host Cailin is pinning her hopes of winning on a Eurovision extravaganza.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit is to The Swan Inn in Midhurst, West Sussex, run by Digby Furneaux. A sunny breakfast leaves the guests satisfied, but Cleide feels rather exposed by the bathroom window.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Osterley Park Hotel in Isleworth, host Sunny Pharroda is joined by his colleague, Faraah Payenda. All the guests complain about a cleaning catastrophe and Digby makes an early check out.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Cleide Brook hosts at Plover Cottage in Huddersfield. The guests are amazed by the space, but Liz and Gary find more than they bargained for in their room, leading to some rude feedback.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Forge Guest Rooms in Long Whatton, Leicestershire, owned by Liz and Gary Scottorn. The guests love the lux, but Cleide questions eating breakfast beside a hot tub.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests meet for the last time to find out what theyve been paid. Criticisms are all laid bare, as Digby reveals how much he paid for a room he didnt even spend the night in.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz head to Norfolk where a sporty French hot hatch awaits. The car belongs to student Senna, who comes from a family of petrol heads.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz are picking up one of Frances most legendary rally racers: a 1972 Renault Alpine 110. The car belongs to former restaurateur Guido.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are helping two sets of first time buyers struggling to find a home in Southampton and The New Forest.

programme.desc: The property gurus are in Larne in Northern Ireland to help a couple decide whether to ditch their rural 70s bungalow or stick with it. Could a garage hold the key?

programme.desc: From fractures, breaks and dislocations to illness brought on mental anguish, the staff at Kings reflect on the different ways in which people deal with physical and emotional pain. programme.title: Yes Sir, I Can Boogie! 1975-1979
programme.desc: Two hours of disco babes who ruled the dancefloors! From Labelle and Lipps Inc to Tina Charles and Sister Sledge - its all girl singers, all the way!

programme.title: Tonys 25 Biggest Pop Groups!
programme.desc: Pop groups ruled the 70s and came from all quarters. From Germanys Boney M, Swedens Abba and the UKs Queen, Police and Showaddywaddy, Tony Blackburn counts down the definitive 25 biggest pop groups.

programme.title: Seven Inch Heroes! 1970-1979
programme.desc: Fifty songs from the cooler end of the 70s spectrum - designed for repeated plays! From Free to The Police via Thin Lizzy, The Clash and Lene Lovich.

programme.title: Power Pop Day!
programme.desc: Non-stop 70s rock and power ballads - all day!

programme.title: The Police! 1978-1981

programme.title: Power Pop Day!
programme.desc: Non-stop 70s rock and power ballads - all day!

programme.title: Clare Grogans Teenage Kicks!
programme.desc: The 5 years from 77 to 81 was possibly the most exciting and vibrant time to be a teenager and Clare Grogan was there - buying the records, seeing the bands and wearing the t-shirts!

programme.title: Power Pop Day!
programme.desc: Non-stop 70s rock and power ballads - all day!

programme.title: Roof Down, Radio UP! Road Trip 50
programme.desc: Cruising down the highway with four hours of 1970s driving songs, featuring some of the coolest FM rock ever, from legends like Fleetwood Mac, Eagles, Elton, ELO, Toto, and many more.

programme.title: Power Pop Day!
programme.desc: Non-stop 70s rock and power ballads - all day!

programme.title: Roskos Hellraisers! Top 25
programme.desc: A music video playlist showcasing the biggest rock and roll rebels of the 1970s, presented by the man who was there for all of it, that groovy host from the west coast, Radio 1 legend, Rosko.

programme.title: Magic Moments! The 70s
programme.desc: Experience the magic of the 70s, where every beat was a spell and every song a timeless enchantment!

programme.title: Magic Moments! The 70s
programme.desc: Experience the magic of the 70s, where every beat was a spell and every song a timeless enchantment!

programme.title: The Now Yearbook 1988
programme.desc: Another top year, with rock, reggae, pop and power ballads rubbing shoulders with exciting new dance sounds from the US and Europe. Bruno Brookes has the story - month by month.

programme.title: 1988 Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1988!

programme.title: 1987s Lesser-Heard-Hits!
programme.desc: You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With Style Council, Boy George, Chris Rea, Billy Idol and Squeeze.

programme.title: Mark Goodiers No.2s of 1987
programme.desc: Sixty minutes of back to back UK chart runners-up from 1987, a year of big hits for Pepsi, Percy and the Pet Shop Boys.

programme.title: 1987 Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1987!

programme.title: Easters Biggest Hits: 1980-1989

programme.title: Spring Has Sprung!
programme.desc: All 80s - 24/7 - the feelgood pop decade where the Brits invaded the world!

programme.title: As Seen On TV with Robin Banks
programme.desc: Robin Banks chart with big TV connections - could be theme tunes, Comic Relief, adverts or just a song made famous by a TV show. Our top 25 chart is Absolutely Fabulous!

programme.title: One Massive Hit: Then Zilch!
programme.desc: They all had one defining chart smash, then never darkened our top 20 again. Here are 40 one hit wonders from Spagna, Black, Robin Beck, Men at Work and many more.

programme.title: Spring Has Sprung!
programme.desc: All 80s - 24/7 - the feelgood pop decade where the Brits invaded the world!

programme.title: 40 One-Hit Wonders with Pat Sharp
programme.desc: They all had one defining chart smash - then never darkened our top 20 again! Here are 40 One Hit Wonders.

programme.title: 1986s Lesser-Heard-Hits!
programme.desc: You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With New Order, Queen, Depeche Mode and Janet Jackson.

programme.title: 40 Years On - 1986s Best Sellers!
programme.desc: A complete rundown of the 30 biggest selling UK singles for 1986 - can you believe these were all hits 40 years ago? And if you thought Madonna was at number 1 - think again!

programme.title: 1986s Lesser-Heard-Hits!
programme.desc: You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With New Order, Queen, Depeche Mode and Janet Jackson.

programme.title: 1986s One-Hit-Wonders! Top 12

programme.desc: The quest for the Holy Grail has consumed knights, Crusaders and Hollywood alike. One church in Spain says they have found the secret chalice, with two ancient Arabic parchments as proof.

programme.title: Revolutionary War Weapons
programme.desc: Discover the key military technologies that helped propel the colonies to victory, from the musket to the first military submarine. From NOVA.

programme.title: The War
programme.desc: In Italy, Allied forces are stalled in the mountains south of Rome, unable to break through the German lines at Monte Cassino. The killing goes on all winter and spring as the enemy manages to fight off repeated Allied attacks. A risky landing at Anzio ends in utter failure; thousands of Allied troops, including Babe Ciarlo of Waterbury, are exposed to enemy fire and unable to advance for months.

programme.title: Ed Sheeran - Live in London

programme.desc: Caravaggios Comb brings us a revised vision of history: the trade routes that brought ivory to Europe in the 16th century. In the foreground of Caravaggios Martha and Mary Magdalene lies a fine ivory comb with a broken tooth.

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger
programme.desc: A janitor becomes a social outcast when a freak accident at a chemical factory turns him into a mutant. However, he soon uses his newfound superhuman strength to battle slimy criminals and a corrupt CEO.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: 2 Fast 2 Furious
programme.desc: To clear his criminal record a former LA cop must infiltrate the Miami street-racing scene to set up a drug baron. Burning rubber all over the city with his ex-con friend, he attempts to deliver the crook to the FBI without crashing and being blown to pieces in one of the many explosions.

programme.title: Clash of the Titans
programme.desc: Perseus travels the underworld to defeat the evil god Hades, who seeks to overthrow Zeus and bring hell to Earth.

programme.title: Wrath of the Titans
programme.desc: Perseus enlists the aid of Queen Andromeda, Hephaestus and Poseidons son to rescue Zeus from the underworld, defeat the Titans and save mankind.

programme.title: Venom: The Last Dance
programme.desc: Eddie Brock and Venom must make a devastating decision as theyre pursued by a mysterious military man and alien monsters from Venoms home world.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 5
programme.desc: In Rio de Janeiro, ex-con Dom Torretto and ex-cop Brian OConner join forces against a corrupt businessman who wants them both dead.

programme.title: Fast and Furious Presents: Hobbs and Shaw
programme.desc: Brixton Lorr is a cybernetically enhanced soldier who possesses superhuman strength, a brilliant mind and a lethal pathogen that could wipe out half of the worlds population. Its now up to hulking lawman Luke Hobbs and lawless operative Deckard Shaw to put aside their past differences and work together to prevent the seemingly indestructible Lorr from destroying humanity.

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man
programme.desc: Peter Parker finds a clue that might help him understand why his parents disappeared when he was young. His path puts him on a collision course with Dr. Curt Connors, his fathers former partner.

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
programme.desc: The emergence of a powerful new villain and the return of an old friend bring Peter Parker to the realization that all his enemies have one thing in common: Oscorp. Based on Marvel Comics characters.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 6
programme.desc: Since Dom and Brians heist in Rio they must live as fugitives. Meanwhile, Agent Hobbs has been tracking a gang of lethally skilled mercenary drivers. Hobbs asks Dom and his crew for help in exchange for full pardons for everyone.

programme.title: William Tell
programme.desc: In 14th Century Switzerland, a once peaceful hunter leads his people in rebellion after his family and country are threatened by a tyrannical Austrian King. As the Holy Roman Empire crumbles, the quiet lives of the Swiss peasants are upended.

programme.title: William Tell
programme.desc: In 14th Century Switzerland, a once peaceful hunter leads his people in rebellion after his family and country are threatened by a tyrannical Austrian King. As the Holy Roman Empire crumbles, the quiet lives of the Swiss peasants are upended.

programme.title: Super-Charlie
programme.desc: A young boy attempts to solve an ongoing series of crimes with the help of his super-powered baby brother.

programme.title: DC Super Hero Girls: Legends of Atlantis
programme.desc: Siren and Mera steal the powerful Book of Legends. The Super Hero Girls head to Atlantis to recover the book before Siren can use it to create an army and take over the entire ocean.

programme.title: Batman Unlimited: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: When the beast-themed Animilitia plague Gotham, Batman swings out to investigate. But with The Penguin and his cyber-animals added to the mix, Batman will need the help of Green Arrow and The Flash to bring them to heel.

programme.title: Dragon Rider
programme.desc: A dragon, an orphaned boy, and a mountain spirit work together as they trek through the Himalayas in search for the Rim of Heaven.

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens
programme.desc: When an evil alien attacks the Earth, a motley crew of monsters is called into action to save the world from imminent destruction. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: DC League of Super-Pets
programme.desc: Krypto the Super-Dog and Superman are inseparable best friends, now Krypto must convince a ragtag group of animals to master their own newfound powers to help him save Superman and the rest of the Justice League.

programme.title: Despicable Me 4
programme.desc: Gru welcomes a new member to the family, Gru Jr., whos intent on tormenting his dad. However, their peaceful existence soon comes crashing down when criminal mastermind Maxime Le Mal escapes from prison and vows revenge against Gru.

programme.title: Fireheart
programme.desc: In New York City in 1932, women are not allowed to be firefighters. When the citys firemen disappear in mysterious fires, Georgia Nolan sees a golden opportunity. She disguises herself as Joe and joins a team of improvised firemen.

programme.title: Shrek
programme.desc: The story of a reclusive green ogre called Shrek whose life of solitude is disrupted by a horde of refugees who have been banished by the evil Lord Farquaad. Determined to save his home, Shrek cuts a deal with the tyrant, who sets him a daunting task - if Shrek is ever to enjoy peace and quiet again, he must rescue Farquaads bride-to-be from a dangerous castle.

programme.title: Shrek 2
programme.desc: Ogre Shrek and his new wife Fiona are surprised to discover a letter from her parents, inviting them over for dinner. Fiona is eager to show off her new husband, but her parents dont yet know shes an ogre. Taking Donkey along, they set off for Far Far Away to visit them, but the trip is hampered by the jealousy of a wicked fairy godmother and her hired assassin, Puss in Boots.

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
programme.desc: The sequel to `Madagascar, in which New York Zoo animals, Alex the Lion, Marty the Zebra, Melman the Giraffe and Gloria the Hippo, still stranded on Madagascar, start to leave the island. All of a sudden, they land in the wilderness of Africa, where Alex meets the rest of his family, but has trouble communicating with them after spending so much time at the Central Park Zoo.

programme.title: The Wild Robot
programme.desc: Shipwrecked on a deserted island, a robot named Roz must learn to adapt to its new surroundings. Building relationships with the native animals, Roz soon develops a parental bond with an orphaned gosling.

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie
programme.desc: Flightless birds lead a mostly happy existence, except for Red, who just cant get past the daily annoyances of life. His temperament leads him to anger management class, where he meets fellow misfits Chuck and Bomb. Red becomes even more agitated when his feathered brethren welcome green pigs to their island paradise. As the swine begin to get under his skin, Red joins forces with Chuck and Bomb to investigate the real reason behind their mysterious arrival.

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie 2
programme.desc: Red, Chuck, Bomb and the rest of their feathered friends are surprised when a green pig suggests that they put aside their differences and unite to fight a common threat. Aggressive birds from an island covered in ice are planning to use an elaborate weapon to destroy the fowl and swine way of life. After picking their best and brightest, the birds and pigs come up with a scheme to infiltrate the island, deactivate the device and return to their respective paradises intact.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! The Sword and the Scoob
programme.desc: An evil sorceress transports the gang back to the age chivalrous knights, spell-casting wizards, and fire-breathing dragons.

programme.title: Scary Girl
programme.desc: To save her father from a dangerous scientist in a world shrouded in darkness, and prevent her planets destruction, a young girl overcomes her fears to travel to a mysterious city of light.

programme.title: Scary Girl
programme.desc: To save her father from a dangerous scientist in a world shrouded in darkness, and prevent her planets destruction, a young girl overcomes her fears to travel to a mysterious city of light.

programme.title: The Naked Gun
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of his bumbling father, Detective Frank Drebin Jr. must solve a murder case to prevent Police Squad from shutting down.

programme.title: The Hangover
programme.desc: A crazy Las Vegas bachelor party turns into a race against time when three hung-over groomsmen awaken after a night of drunken debauchery to find that the groom has gone missing, and attempt to get him to the alter in time for his wedding.

programme.title: The Hangover Part II
programme.desc: A group of friends go to Thailand for a quiet and restrained stag party, but are dismayed to wake up in a mysterious hotel room, with a man missing and no memory of the night before. Of course, Alan manages to get himself in with the crowd.

programme.title: The Hangover Part III
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Doug take Alan on a road trip to help him through a personal crisis.

programme.title: Hope Springs
programme.desc: A woman drags her skeptical husband to a renowned counsellors marriage retreat to try to put the spark back in their relationship.

programme.title: Youve Got Mail
programme.desc: Two rival bookstore owners are unaware that they are conducting a secret romance with each other on the Internet. When they discover the truth, they must prevent their business rivalry from interfering with their growing personal intimacy.

programme.title: Mamma Mia!
programme.desc: Set on a Greek island, a young woman who is about to be married suspects that any one of three men could be her father. She invites them all to her wedding without telling her mother. Movie adaptation of the musical, set to Abbas greatest hits.

programme.title: Yesterday
programme.desc: Jack Malik is a struggling singer-songwriter in an English seaside town whose dreams of fame are rapidly fading, despite the fierce devotion and support of his childhood best friend, Ellie. After a freak bus accident during a mysterious global blackout, Jack wakes up to discover that The Beatles have never existed. Performing songs by the greatest band in history to a world that has never heard them, Jack becomes on overnight sensation with a little help from his agent.

programme.title: Mean Girls
programme.desc: After 15 years in African bush country, Cady knows all about herd instincts, but nothing has prepared her for high school. She joins a popular but cruelly manipulative crowd of girls known as the `Plastics and secretly mocks them, but the fun stops when one betrays her and steals the boy she fancies.

programme.title: The Whole Nine Yards
programme.desc: The wife of a dentist convinces him to betray their new neighbour - a hit man turned informant - to the Chicago gangsters who want him dead. In need of money in order to hurry through a divorce, the young man arranges a meeting with the Mob in which he will exchange information for cash, but when his scheming wife tells the hit man what he is up to, it is the dentists life that is placed in grave danger.

programme.title: Little Fockers
programme.desc: Greg and Pam have been together for ten years, and now theyre the proud parents of two adorable twins. However, money is tight and in order to keep his family afloat, devoted nurse Greg takes a second job working for a drug company. That development doesnt sit well with Jack, whose previous suspicions regarding his hapless son-in-law soon come back in full force after learning of this development.

programme.title: Daddys Home 2
programme.desc: Brad and Dustys respective fathers show up to turn Christmastime upside down.

programme.title: One More Shot
programme.desc: On New Years Eve 1999, Minnie discovers a bottle of tequila that allows her to travel through time, each shot taking her back to the start of the night, giving her the chance to alter the course of her millennium.

programme.title: The Phoenician Scheme
programme.desc: Wealthy businessman, Zsa-zsa Korda appoints his only daughter, a nun, as sole heir to his estate. As Korda embarks on a new enterprise, they soon become the target of scheming tycoons, foreign terrorists, and determined assassins.

programme.title: The First Wives Club
programme.desc: When Brenda Cushman, Elise Elliot and Annie Paradis are reunited at the funeral of a college friend, they discover they have another thing in common: theyve all been dumped by their husbands, Morty, Bill and Aaron, for a younger model. Vowing revenge, they decide to hit them where it hurts most: the pocket. Using help and their various skills, they begin to pick them off one by on.

programme.title: The First Wives Club

programme.title: No Escape
programme.desc: In Southeast Asia, a newly relocated American businessman and his family look for a safe haven during a violent political uprising.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Risky Romance
programme.desc: From killers and zombies to forbidden love and dangerous desires, `The Cinema List explores romances where passion courts danger and love is always worth the risk.

programme.title: Jackie
programme.desc: The days leading up to the tragedy of President John F. Kennedys assassination and its immediate aftermath are chronicled as they were experienced by the First Lady, wife of the charismatic president and mother of two small children.

programme.title: Quartet
programme.desc: A long-smouldering flame is rekindled when a once-popular opera star reunites with her ex-husband and fellow performers at a retirement home for musicians.

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption
programme.desc: Andy Dufresne and Ellis Boyd `Red Redding are two prisoners serving long sentences in the vicious Shawshank jail. The two come to terms with their imprisonment while surviving the sadistic acts of prison warden Samuel Norton, and for both, a different sort of redemption awaits.

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket
programme.desc: A US Marine observes the effects the Vietnam War has on his fellow Marines from their brutal basic training to the bloody street fighting in Hue, Vietnam.

programme.title: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Following a group of Los Angeles residents who try to find their way through the highs and lows of love during a single day.

programme.title: No Escape
programme.desc: In Southeast Asia, a newly relocated American businessman and his family look for a safe haven during a violent political uprising.

programme.title: Jackie
programme.desc: The days leading up to the tragedy of President John F. Kennedys assassination and its immediate aftermath are chronicled as they were experienced by the First Lady, wife of the charismatic president and mother of two small children.

programme.title: Elizabeth: The Golden Age
programme.desc: Elizabeth might have her royal rival Mary Queen of Scots in custody, but a far greater threat lies to the east: the Spanish Armada. Despite her reign of 30 years, Elizabeths hold on the English throne remains uncertain. Her kingdom lies in the shadow of mighty Spain, Philip IIs vast army and the Inquisition, but Elizabeth is undaunted.

programme.title: It Ends With Us
programme.desc: Lily Bloom falls for a charming neurosurgeon after she moves to Boston to chase her dream of opening her own business. However, when her first love suddenly reenters her life, her relationship gets upended, leaving her with an impossible choice.

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri
programme.desc: A conflicted Navy pilot and veteran of the Second World War questions the role he is forced to play when he is called back into service for the Korean War.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby and his dying mother. Her dreary life is interrupted when her schoolgirl crush arrives back in town.

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby and his dying mother. Her dreary life is interrupted when her schoolgirl crush arrives back in town.

programme.title: The Tiger Rising
programme.desc: When a boy discovers a caged tiger in the woods near his home, his imagination runs wild as his life begins to change in unexpected ways.

programme.title: It Takes Two
programme.desc: When a foster child meets the daughter of a bazillionaire, they hatch a plan to solve the problems in their life, and switch identities.

programme.title: Harold and the Purple Crayon
programme.desc: Inside his book, Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the books pages and into the real world, he learns his trusty crayon can set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible.

programme.title: Boonie Bears: Guardian Code
programme.desc: Briar and Bramble lose their mother at a very young age under mysterious circumstances. Many years later, logger Vick takes the bears to visit the Robot Research Institute, and they uncover a clue relating to their mother.

programme.title: Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: The plucky penguins join a special inter-species task force in an attempt to stop an evil doctor from taking over the world.

programme.title: Transformers One
programme.desc: Brothers-in-arms Orion Pax and D-16 become sworn enemies Optimus Prime and Megatron.

programme.title: The Emoji Movie
programme.desc: Three emojis embark on an epic adventure through a smartphone to save their world from being deleted.

programme.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
programme.desc: Charlie Bucket comes from a poor family, and spends most of his time dreaming about the chocolate that he loves but usually cant afford. Things change when Willy Wonka, head of the very popular Wonka Chocolate empire, announces a contest in which five gold tickets have been hidden in chocolate bars. Charlie is one of the five kids who finds a lucky ticket and goes on a tour of Wonkas chocolate factory.

programme.title: The Golden Compass
programme.desc: In a parallel universe, young orphan Lyra Belacqua resides in Oxford under the patronage of her dashing adventurer Uncle, Lord Asriel. When Lyra comes into possession of an alethiometer, a compass that truthfully answers any question, there are people who will do anything to get hold of it, and Lyra finds herself on an epic quest to the frozen North to safeguard the future of her entire world.

programme.title: The Smurfs 2
programme.desc: Papa and the rest of the Smurfs reunite with their human friends to rescue Smurfette from the clutches of evil wizard Gargamel.

programme.title: Harold and the Purple Crayon

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug
Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens
programme.desc: When an evil alien attacks the Earth, a motley crew of monsters is called into action to save the world from imminent destruction. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: The Lost Boys
programme.desc: Fang-in-cheek tale about a single-parent family who move to the coastal town of Santa Clara where the local juvenile delinquents are actually a pack of vampires.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: The Shining
programme.desc: Frustrated writer Jack Torrance takes a job as the winter caretaker at the ominous, mountain-locked Overlook Hotel so that he can write in peace. When he arrives there with his wife and son, they learn that the previous caretaker had gone mad. Slowly Jack becomes possessed by the evil, demonic presence in the hotel.

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri
programme.desc: A conflicted Navy pilot and veteran of the Second World War questions the role he is forced to play when he is called back into service for the Korean War.

programme.title: Hondo
programme.desc: An embittered Indian scout attempts to escort a homesteaders wife and her son back to safety after her cowardly husband deserts her during an Apache attack. When she refuses to go, the scout stays on to help for a while and, over time, forms a strong bond with both her and her son.

programme.title: The Warriors
programme.desc: Wrongly accused of assassinating top gang leader Cyrus at a rally, Coney Islanders The Warriors have to fight their way home over the course of one long, violent night.

programme.title: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: On a remote island, a wealthy entrepreneur creates a theme park featuring living dinosaurs drawn from prehistoric DNA. Before opening the attraction to the public, he invites a top palaeontologist, palaeobotanist, mathematician/theorist, and his two eager grandchildren to experience the park. Their park visit is anything but tranquil as the parks security system breaks down, and the prehistoric creatures break out.

programme.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
programme.desc: Epic tale in which an intrepid archaeologist tries to beat a band of Nazis to a unique religious relic which is central to their plans for world domination. Battling against a snake phobia and a vengeful ex-girlfriend, Indiana Jones is in constant peril, making hairs-breadth escapes at every turn in this celebration of the innocent adventure movies of an earlier era.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: Adventure sequel. Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones, on the trail of fortune and glory in Old Shanghai, is ricocheted into a dangerous adventure in India. With his faithful companion Short Round and nightclub singer Willie Scott, Indie goes in search of the magical Sankara stone, and uncovers an ancient evil which threatens all who come into contact with it.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer Indiana Jones sets out to rescue his father, a medievalist who has vanished while searching for the Holy Grail. Following clues in the old mans notebook, Indy arrives in Venice, where he enlists the help of a beautiful academic, but they are not the only ones who are on the trail, and some sinister old enemies soon come out of the woodwork.

programme.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: Interview With the Vampire
programme.desc: When reporter Daniel Malloys curiosity leads him to interview the mysterious Louis De Pointe Du Lac, the last thing he expects is to find himself talking to a vampire. Louis tells him his life story, about how he was turned, and the vampire Lestat who made him what he is.

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: The disturbing story of the making of the infamous 60s underworld figures Reggie and Ronnie Kray - from their upbringing by a domineering mother in the deprivation of Londons East End. The gangland empire they build is characterised by violence and intimidation, and the film is unflinching in its depiction of their rise to power.

His brother Kip has an online relationship and dreams of being a cage fighter, while his uncle Rico is more interested in his mail-order time-travel machine. Napoleons mission is to help his friend Pedro win the student presidency from the nasty Summer.

programme.title: Blades of Glory
programme.desc: Chazz Michael Michaels and Jimmy MacElroy are the two titans of skating. At the Winter Olympics, the fierce rivals find themselves forced to share the winners podium when they both score gold. The shower of glory doesnt last for long, as the pair descend into fisticuffs and set the mascot on fire. Disgraced and stripped of their medals, they can get back on ice, but only if they dance with each other as a pair.

They hope to take in one small child, but when they meet three siblings, including a rebellious 15-year-old girl, they find themselves speeding from zero to three kids overnight. Now, Pete and Ellie must try to learn the ropes of instant parenthood in the hope of becoming a family.

programme.desc: Daredevils go for the gross-out when they subject an unsuspecting public to more outrageous stunts and practical jokes.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Forged in Fire

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: When a trio of Elephants are orphaned in Zambia, they are rescued and transported to a purpose-build nursery. But it wont be smooth-sailing for the young Pachyderms; life lessons will be tough to imprint before they are returned to the wild.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: A two-year-old orangutan who was drugged and kept as an illegal pet by an oil sheik arrives at jungle school. Plus, angry male orangutan Jerry has gone missing and has not been seen for 18 days.

programme.desc: New loco manager Piglet is once more in the thick of it with engine problems.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The Republic P-47 Thunderbolt proved to be a successful high-altitude fighter during the Second World War, and it also became Americas foremost fighter-bomber in the ground-attack role.

programme.title: Hunting Nazi Treasure
programme.desc: The team searches for $1.5 billion in stolen gold from the Bank of Italy, and the missing art from Florences museums.

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Who Put Bella in the Wych Elm?
programme.desc: The dark tale of the skeleton of a woman found in a hollowed-out tree in Birmingham woodland, exploring the links between her and Nazi spy rings in Britain.

programme.title: Michael Aspels WWII
programme.desc: Its 1938, Germany invades Czechoslovakia and Britain introduces conscription. Sandbags appear throughout London. Poland is invaded and Britain declares war. Narrated by Michael Aspel.

programme.title: Last Voices of World War I
programme.desc: Testimonials from more than 100 WWI veterans in interviews captured during the nineties and 2000s, along with historic newsreel footage and dramatic reconstructions.

programme.title: Living in the Shadow of World War Two
programme.desc: With bombing campaigns waged by both sides, civilian casualties far outnumbered those of military personnel during the war. Citizens took action during the Blitz to protect themselves.

programme.title: WW1 Battles
programme.desc: The Gallipoli campaign was the land-based part of a strategy intended to allow Allied ships to pass through the Dardanelles, capture Constantinople, and ultimately knock Ottoman Turkey out of the war.

programme.title: Where Were You?
programme.desc: Following the 1969 moon landing, exploration of the lunar surface continued. Apollo 12 collected earth and rock samples, and soon the next mission, Apollo 13, was also set to reach the moon.

programme.title: World War I: The War to End All Wars
programme.desc: Combat at sea and beyond Europe turns the war into a truly global conflict.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The definitive combat helicopter saw first blood in South East Asia and has been transport, gunship, smoke ship, command centre, MediVac and dozens of other roles.

programme.title: Greatest Unsolved Mysteries

programme.desc: The drug squad arrest three men for dealing cocaine - however they will not come easily. The squad also find hash in a bush at a beauty spot, and a suspect runs away from a road check.

programme.title: Cops on the Rock
programme.desc: Customs officers rescue a man from the sea at night after he falls from a smugglers boat, and the Drug Squad raid a suspected dealers house.

programme.title: Railroad Australia
programme.desc: A re-tooled grain train is springing leaks. Bernies Streamliner festival faces disaster and the Pichi Richi Explorer takes part in a crazy race in South Australia.

programme.title: Rick Steins Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: On a misty lagoon in Sardinia, Rick watches the fishermen catch mullet using the same methods as the Romans did centuries before. He boards a ferry for Sicily to find, in the heart of Palermo, one of the best pasta dishes hes ever tasted.

programme.title: Borderforce: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Money and weapons belonging to a cartel are found in a car. An illegal shipment of exotic reptiles is discovered inside a truck carrying used clothing. A man is caught smuggling medicine for his sick family. And a murderer is extradited to Mexico.

programme.title: Borderforce: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: The bridges on the southern border of the United States are closed and allow only essential vehicles to pass through due to the pandemic. However, the fight against the Mexican cartels continues more intense than ever.

programme.title: Guy Martin: Proper Jobs
programme.desc: Guy goes to work one mile below the North Sea in the UKs last deep mine.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to end the chaos and misery that snowfall brings to Britain every year, using a combine harvester, some basic engineering skills and a quick trip to Norway. Also in this show, Jeremy is on the test track in the new Audi RS5 and the latest BMW M3. Plus theres a rather surprising guest in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In the last show of this series, Jeremy Clarkson races the new Jaguar XJ against something even bigger and more powerful - the rotation of the Earth itself. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond achieves a boyhood dream by testing two 1980s supercar icons, the Porsche 959 and Ferrari F40, and James May is in America to drive NASAs very latest Space Exploration Vehicle.

programme.desc: Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster host as more celebrities attempt to put the unreal into reality - their guests this time are Bill Bailey, Jo Brand, Harriet Kemsley and Darren Harriott.

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life is Goodish

programme.title: Cops On The Rock
programme.sub-title: Its Not All Rainbows And Butterflies
programme.desc: Armed police and drug squad officers arrest three men after a dangerous social media posting, and a man fails a breathalyser after crashing his moped.

programme.title: Cops on the Rock
programme.desc: Customs officers fish hash bales out of the sea and incinerate them, discarded smugglers jerry cans wash up on a beach, and cops stop a car laden with extra fuel.

programme.title: Railroad Australia
programme.desc: In Victoria, a crack team race to build the states VLocity trains of the future. Slippery tracks and kangaroos throw a 5000 tonne coal train off its path to port.

programme.title: Rick Steins Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: In Sicily Rick enjoys the colour and the sheer operatic quality of Catanias busy fish market. In Puglia, on Italys mainland, he discovers great dishes formed through centuries of poverty but wakes up one morning to find his Land Rover missing!

programme.title: Borderforce: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Thousands of dollars from a cartel are found hidden inside an engine. A 17-year-old boy is intoxicated and needs urgent medical attention. Large quantities of cocaine appear in rubbish bags. And a man tries to enter the US with a false visa.

programme.title: Borderforce: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Cocaine is found inside the walls of a refrigerated truck. A traveller tries to smuggle weapons into Mexico. A Honduran immigrant, fleeing gang violence, seeks asylum in the US. And a father is caught trying to smuggle medicine for his son.

programme.title: Guy Martin: Proper Jobs
programme.desc: Motorcycle racer, truck fitter, part-time farmer, enthusiastic engineer, and sometimes television presenter, Guy Martin attempts a variety of different roles that involve hard graft and no end of skill.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th birthday of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern, £500,000 interpretation of this classic sports car before putting on a typically low key birthday party involving live music, classic fighter planes and a group of Royal Marines. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond is in South Africa to test a rugged alternative to the famous Hummer.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Italy to take part in a series of tricky challenges and increasingly loud arguments as they attempt to find the worlds best hot hatchback. Clarkson is in the Citroen DS3 Racing, Hammond championing the Fiat 500C Abarth and May making the case for the Renaultsport Clio Cup. Petrolhead comedian Ross Noble is the `Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.desc: Dave and Si explore how our love affair with beer and cider has evolved over centuries. At one time it was safer to drink beer than water and the Bikers visit a cider festival, where they press their own.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Mother of All Mistakes - Or Is It?
programme.desc: Adopted as a baby, former milkman and undercover secret agent Hobbo is determined to trace his natural mother. He has found out her name and exactly where she lives, but how should he approach the poor woman to tell her the good news? Meanwhile, Howard is in trouble when Marina, upon finding out that he has been making enquiries about another woman, turns up at his house.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will Howard Cross the Atlantic Single-Handed?
programme.desc: Hobbo is still having trouble getting Nelly to admit that she is his natural birth mother. To get the proof he needs, he fits Howard up with an eavesdropping wire to pick up Nelly talking to Pearl. Howard does his best to look inconspicuous, but his rumbling stomach and an electronic fault on his transmitter may ruin his plans.

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Deed is approached by a woman who claims that her entire family was wiped out in Iraq by the depleted uranium used by the British in their tank shells. Despite problems in their friendship, and her approaching wedding, Deed persuades Jo Mills to take on the case. Deed also presides over a case brought by a victim of motor neurone disease, who claims that her illness was caused by the installation of a mobile phone mast.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigate the mysterious case of the ice cream bandit, an armed robber who targeted the vans of two feuding ice cream manufacturers in the mid 1990s. When the battle between the two families erupts into violence, UCOS decide to track down the bandit and lay the conflict to rest once and for all.

Another danger arises when he demonstrates his gift of disguise, and finds himself the new recipient of Marinas affections. Hobbo then discovers the danger of exercising when his neck locks solid while doing some simple head movements.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Nobody Messes With Tony the Throat
programme.desc: Howard is deeply concerned when Pearl starts going out every day with Nelly, and she will not say where. Hobbo comes to his aid, and heads a surveillance party to follow Pearl and Nelly. When the women go into a Hells Angels club, they decide not to be hasty, and confront them later.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will Stella Find True Love with Norris Fairburn?
programme.desc: When Clegg and Truly tell Hobbo that local serial groom Norris Fairburn is free again, he decides that Norris would be an ideal husband for Stella. programme.desc: Halloween gets spookier when a ghost tour guide sees a body being buried on the beach, but when police uncover nothing, Pearl is on the lookout for a missing body.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The latest round of motoring news sees Jeremy reporting on the Porsche GT3 RS and Ferrari 360 Challenge Stradale, and Richard searching for a Dodge Charger. The presenters try to find a road legal car for less than £100, and Jordan is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin starts his new job in Cardiff and Stacey is thrilled to be at home again. Smithy comes to terms with life in Essex without his best mate, whilst Nessa is adjusting to life in Daves caravan down Sully.
