programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats handsome Dobermann Dexter, who has a serious spinal condition, feisty feline Driver, whos been hit by a car, and sixteen-month-old Newfoundland Bailey, who has developmental elbow disease.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats eight-year-old Dalmatian Milo, whose squashed spinal cord is causing him constant pain. And Noel attempts to fuse four-year-old shih-tzu Ollies elbow in order to save his leg.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats cockapoo Betsy, who has been receiving treatment after being hit by a van five months ago, but now she has gangrene. Also treated is bijou puppy Angel, who has a genetic deformity.

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Gadsden Correctional Facility near Tallahassee in Florida where he meets Ashley Humphrey, who pumped eight bullets into a woman she had never met.

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility where he meets Sheila Davalloo, convicted for the murder of Anna Lisa Raymundo and the attempted murder of her own husband.

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Theres heartbreak and jubilation as Alex meets two couples desperately trying to defy the odds and become parents.

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: In this emotional final episode, Alex comes to the end of her training at Kings and three couples await their results. Will they finally become parents?

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: With purple, green and yellow ribbons flying, and sporting ankle bells, Michael Portillo is led a merry dance in Stogumber, all in the name of fertility. He visits a celebrated Edwardian garden at Hestercombe to discover the fruits of an unusual partnership.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boldly goes to the moon by way of the Cornish Riviera Express! On the trail of an historic achievement made at the dawn of the Edwardian era, he investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins in the East Yorkshire port of Hull, where he heads for the docks through which millions of migrants passed at the turn of the 20th century and which are today getting a second wind. At Cottingham, Michael picks up the trail of the future King Edward VII, when he was Prince of Wales. Bertie, as he was known in the family, became embroiled in scandal after a high stakes game of cards in the grand house of Tranby Croft.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Edwardian Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo conducts important research in an historic tea room, built by an Edwardian immigrant to the city of York. Research of a more sombre kind leads Michael to the roots of the modern welfare state in the work of an early 20th century Quaker investigator, whose family manufactured chocolate.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Up for sale at the Mathewsons auction is a 1275 Mini GT thats come from just down the road and could be a future British icon.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Yorkshire to pick up a veteran London-to-Brighton car, theres an emotional farewell to a 1980s Bedford HA van, and a Toyota Corona finds a new home.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: In the 1960s and 70s, the Second World War is a distant memory, but a few diehards are determined to hunt down thousands of Nazi war criminals. Many of whom are living in plain sight.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Adam Schoon gets a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to see the Scottish Cup, the most important trophy in Scottish football, and he finds out more about a pioneering player who won it in the late 19th century, Andrew Watson, considered the first black international footballer. Geoffrey Munn is surprised by rare Russian royal jewels that arrive in a carrier bag, while Ronnie Archer-Morgan admires an ornately carved Maori `feather box, used to store precious artefacts.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team at Aston Hall in Birmingham, where Fiona meets celebrated poet Benjamin Zephaniah, who grew up nearby and has fond childhood memories of playing in the grounds. Runjeet Singh, a specialist in Asian arms and armour, is thrilled to see some ornate and valuable ceremonial weapons from India, while Paul Atterbury is intrigued to see a rare example of the only artwork ever to be left on the moon.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome. Two Ford Escorts get Daves pulse racing.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husbands treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom builds a drift car with engineer Sam Lovegrove. At home, he gets a digger in to make his new pond, and he and Sam have a go at gig rowing after fixing the club trailer in Hayle.

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: During a career spanning more than fifteen years, Simon has visited over a hundred countries on six different continents. Now, he catches up with some of the memorable characters hes met along the way.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the steel city of Sheffield, where he discovers an enormous and beautifully preserved early 20th century steam engine. At 400 tons and packing 12,000 horsepower, it produced armour plate for the most feared warship of the Royal Navy.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Edwardian railway guide takes him to the city of Liverpool, where King Edward VII had recently laid the foundation stone for a grand Anglican cathedral at the top of St Jamess Mount. The young winner of a competition to design the building was a Roman Catholic from a family of architects. His name was Giles Gilbert Scott.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world, stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry. He uncovers a bitter industrial dispute between quarrymen and the owner of the pit, Lord Penrhyn, which divided the community at the beginning of the 20th century.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his early 20th century Bradshaws Guide, Michael Portillo embarks on a journey through Britains industrial heartland in the footsteps of King George V. He starts in Warrington and learns how the mayor, Dr George Joseph, received the royal party in the parlour of Warringtons magnificent town hall.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek gives the once-over to a Royale Formula Ford race car, and Paul is on a marathon trip to Teesside to pick up a 1953 MGYB, a Moggy Van and an 80s Yamaha 250RD motorbike.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave is on HQ duties and admires a Punto thats two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock.

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Two spies turn double agents at the height of the Cold War standoff between East and West. Their deception happens as the superpowers edge towards nuclear war.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team travel to Portchester Castle in Hampshire on the south coast of England, where treasures include an extraordinary set of RAF medals awarded for extreme bravery to an airman who served in Dunkirk and the Battle of Britain. The team also admire some rare punk clothing by fashion designer Vivienne Westwood and a roll of ornate Arts and Crafts wallpaper destined for the bonfire.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team visit the Ulster Folk Museum near Belfast, where treasures include stained glass, teacups from a luxury ocean liner and an unusual piece of medical history.
